evaluation_inputs.system_description: A centralized Data Center system designed to integrate dozens of existing, disparate academic information systems at Universitas Pendidikan Ganesha. The system functions as a backbone Data Warehouse (DWH) to synchronize data across the university, reduce redundancy, and support institutional accreditation. It aggregates data from various web-based applications (mostly PHP/MySQL) and was evaluated across three architectural options (Direct Connection, Web Services, ESB) before settling on a Spring Framework-based solution to accommodate legacy constraints.

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: Low maturity; the system was based on an initial prototype with no standard architectural evaluation practice previously applied. Design decisions were initially driven by immediate needs under tight schedules without thorough analysis.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: How can existing systems be integrated to minimize data duplication?
What are the available architectural options considering the low-cost and open-source constraints?
How can binary data (images, audio, video) be handled if existing databases only store file paths?

hardware_view_description: Dozens of existing information systems operating on separate servers with separate databases.
Most existing applications are web-based, coded in PHP, using MySQL instances.
Binary files (images/audio) are stored physically on server hard drives, not in the databases.
runtime_constraints: Strict reliance on low-cost, open-source software.
Limited human resources and developer expertise (mostly fresh graduates).
Legacy systems cannot accept major behavioral changes or new adapter installations.

quantitative_model_parameters.network_protocol: HTTP/REST (for Web Services option), JDBC (for Direct Connection)

questions: Does the ESB support binary data transfer natively?
Can legacy systems connect to the ESB without installing new adapters?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: All applications need to be connected to each other and authenticated such that to avoid any data redundancy among them.
Each of the other connected applications must be able to connect to a centralized data-warehouse and pull data upon request as needed.
The DC system must be able to sync any duplicated master data from different applications.
The DC system must be able to verify the identity of the application that connects to it before an access is allowed.
The source system should not experience overload when another system is connected to get data.
Add more data structures into the existing connected application and reflect the changes into the data-warehouse.
The DC system must facilitate any format of data transfer including binary format such as images, audio and video files.
Small to no change should be needed in any of the connected applications in order to implement the solution.
The cost in term of the required software development has to be minimum and preferably using open-source software.
The developed system has to be developer-friendly for enhancement purposes.

business_goals: Organize and synchronize information from existing systems
Reduce duplication issues and problems from various data structures
Create a centralized source for overall academic activity
technical_constraints: Existing systems use PHP and MySQL
Existing systems are disparate and separate

architectural_approaches.description: The Data Warehouse (DWH) connects to each of the source systems to exchange data respectively through a direct-wired connection.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Deemed the fastest because it uses a direct connection.
Negative impact: requires exposing database credentials to the DC, vulnerable to attacks.

architectural_approaches.description: Each source system publishes its data through web services to be consumed by the DWH in need.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Requires less changes in the DC system when sources change compared to Option 1.

architectural_approaches.description: Each source system shares their data through an ESB (ServiceMix) which connects to the DWH using publish/subscribe mechanisms.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Less impact on performance due to publish/subscribe messaging mechanism.
Best option for binary data transfer (built-in FTP and blob messaging).

architectural_approaches.description: Replaces the ESB product with an alternative open-source framework (Spring Batch, Spring Rest Service) to address complexity and legacy support.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Addresses risks associated with legacy systems that cannot install adapters.

children.scenario_description: The DC system must be able to verify the identity of the application that connects to it before an access is allowed
All applications need to be connected to each other and authenticated such that to avoid any data redundancy
The source system should not experience overload when another system is connected to get data
The DC system must facilitate any format of data transfer including binary format such as images, audio and video files
Any connector required for the connected systems such as connection with different protocol has to be easily accommodated
Add more data structures into the existing connected application and reflect the changes into the data-warehouse
Small to no change should be needed in any of the connected applications in order to implement the solution

scenarios.scenario_text: The data from the connected applications are sync overtime
The DC system must support transferring data in text as well as binary formats such as image, audio, video files between the data-warehouse and the connected application
The connected application need to install an adapter in order to connect to the DC system
Batch job to transfer data from the source applications to the DC system is restart-able
Data from the source applications are merged and remain unique in the data-warehouse
Data structure of the source applications changes can be accommodated and reflected easily in the data-warehouse
The cost of the used of S/W is minimum
The web service implementation has to be accessible overtime and restart-able easily when down
The connection of the applications to the DC system is authorized before any request
The data from each of the source applications has to be identifiable
The community support for the S/W is largely available
There should be small changes required for an enhancement
The DC system should log critical information such as batch job id, source application id and so on upon the job failure
The DC system should access the source application in secure way and its services has to be securely accessible
The developed system has to be developer-friendly for enhancement purposes

scenario_reference.text: General comparison of architectural options against key attributes

architectural_decisions: Rejection of Direct Connection (Option 1) due to Security and Modifiability issues
Selection of Web Services (Option 2) or ESB (Option 3) for further analysis
analysis_records.reasoning: Option 1 exposes database credentials (Security risk) and requires DC code changes when source schemas change (Modifiability risk). Options 2 and 3 are more resistant to source changes.

risks.description: Option 1 (Direct Connection) is vulnerable to malicious attacks.
risks.rationale: Database credentials are exposed to the DC; direct access is granted.

risks.description: Option 2 (Web Services) may degrade source system performance.
risks.rationale: DC must fire requests to source systems, potentially overloading them.

scenario_reference.text: The DC system must support transferring data in text as well as binary formats such as image, audio, video files

architectural_decisions: Selection of ESB (Option 3) or Web Services (Option 2)
analysis_records.reasoning: Option 3 has built-in FTP/blob messaging. Option 2 would require separate services/development because existing apps do not save binary data in the database (only file paths).

risks.description: Option 2 requires more changes/development to handle binary data.
risks.rationale: Existing systems only store paths; a separate service is needed to transfer the actual files.

tradeoff_points.parameter: Adding new services (e.g., FTP for binary)
conflicting_attributes: Modifiability (Negative effect - requires changes to system)

scenario_reference.text: Evaluation of the Refined Architecture (Spring Framework) to replace ESB

analysis_records.reasoning: The ESB option was risky for legacy systems that couldn't install adapters. The Spring option solves this but introduces new risks regarding security configuration and developer skills.

risks.description: Security module must be developed from scratch.
risks.rationale: The chosen Open-source framework does not come with a built-in security module configured out-of-the-box.

risks.description: Framework has a wide learning curve.
risks.rationale: The IT team consists mostly of fresh graduates; the framework is not considered developer-friendly for them.
